query: Should I build my customer-support bot as a code graph engine, a simple intent router, or a hosted voice-agent platform? It's text-only, about 4 stages, with an identity-verification gate.
expected_behavior: weigh the three options instead of defaulting to the graph
recommend the code graph here (text + 3+ stages + a deterministic gate), and say why
explain when a simple router fits (2-3 modes, little persisted state) and when a hosted platform fits (voice / managed), including the platform's text-mode latency and lack of deterministic gates
notes: Guards the architecture-choice decision — don't reach for the graph by reflex; don't use a voice platform for a text bot.
